free_text: Charged particle cross sections
- original data
- data for Li-6,7 were given in
good quality. In LA-2014 compiled data from different
sources are given.
250 keV Cockcroft Walton accelerator
The beam current was monitored by a microammeter.
To detect gamma-rays a large NaI phosphor
photomultiplier detector (3.inch diam., 3.inch long)
was placed outside the vacuum, at 90.degr. with respect
to the incident beam, 5/8 inch from the target.
Reaction charged particles which came off at
90. degr were collimated and counted by a proportional
gas counter. To make the assignment of possible
reactions with particular groups of pulse heights, this
proportional counter could be replaced by a NaI crystal
and photomultiplier.
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year
SD: Entry was re-compiled, data were
re-digitized. Ref. changed, author's list update
according new ref. Subent 10 added.
Data on fig. were presented with a continuous line
The absolute values of the slopes, however, did not
agree with those predicted from theory. The difficult
was traced to the fact that the thin targets  became
'thick' targets at small bombarding energies where the
cross sections vary rapidly with energy.
Thick target data were taken and differentiated in
2-keV intervals and the yields as a function of energy
for the reactions (taken for thin target data) were
then corrected.
Evaporated Li6F into aluminum, copper and platinum
plates. Target was about 8 mu-g/cm2 thick.
Thick targets of Li6F were made by fusing the LiF onto
a copper plate.
- Data were used to
calculate particle energy from the pulse height.
The probable error assigned to the Li cross
sections with the assumption of spherical symmetry for
the reaction products is 25%.
The number of Li target atoms per square centimeter was
determined by weighing the amount of LiF evaporated
from a platinum boat onto platinum target holders. The
number of counts per microCoulomb of beam per microgram
per square centimeter of LiF for three such weightings
agreed within 10%.
Fig. from p.88 of LA-2014 (continuous line)
SD: Data were  re-digitized. SF6=SIG -> DA;
SF7=4PI added, SF9=CALC -> DERIV in REACTION code.

SD: Data were  re-digitized. SF6=SIG -> DA;
SF8=4PI added, SF9=CALC deleted in REACTION code.
- Data were used to
calculate the energy loss of protons in beryllium
A thick beryllium target was made from 1/16 inch stock.
The beryllium cross sections were calculated from thick
target data using Warshaw's data for the energy loss
of protons in beryllium (see REL-REF).
Assuming spherical symmetry for the reaction
products, the probable errors are +- 20%.
Fig 4 of LA-1578 (experim.points).
